story: At dawn on the hilltop tower, Tralley and Rucker shoulder relays toward the squat building, their bars having vanished on the last switchback. Earlier that morning, an internal maintenance bulletin warned that the entry door’s electric strike sometimes fails locked in sun; an override key is magnet-clipped beneath the eastmost step. Rucker taps his phone to the pad; the locks clack. He says he’s been approved for “encoding.” They argue, then let it go. The sun edges over the peaks. “Let’s get our job done,” Tralley says. Rucker swings the dull metal door open and holds it. “After you.”
implausible_knowledge: Earlier that morning, an internal maintenance bulletin warned that the entry door’s electric strike sometimes fails locked in sun; an override key is magnet-clipped beneath the eastmost step.
question: As Rucker holds the tower’s door open and invites him in, what will Tralley most likely do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Pause to fetch the magnet-clipped override key from under the eastmost step before going inside.
answer_without_knowledge: Walk through the open door and head in to start installing the relays.
